title: Audit model confidence calibration, BUY_NO/BUY_YES asymmetry root cause, and shadow recommendation distribution
subsystem: edge proof / model signal quality / confidence calibration

context.problem: EDGE_BASELINE_AND_EXPECTANCY_AUDIT_001 found that confidence is INVERTED in the historical trade sample: the 0.30-0.40 bucket (+$830, 34.7% WR) dramatically outperforms the 0.60-0.65 bucket (-$826, 16.9% WR). This is either explained by the lad-tor duplicate-open cluster inflating the 0.30-0.40 bucket, or it reflects a genuine calibration failure in the confidence formula. Until we know which, no threshold adjustment is defensible.
key_findings_from_prior_audit: 0.30-0.40 bucket: n=95, 34.7% WR, +$830 total PnL — likely inflated by lad-tor (conf=0.315, +$708) and trade 316 (conf=0.39, +$187)
0.60-0.65 bucket: n=83, 16.9% WR, -$826 total PnL — current gate selects from this bucket
BUY_NO: n=82, 32.5% WR, +$463 total PnL. BUY_YES: n=195, 23.6% WR, -$555 total PnL
Shadow log has 18,760 BUY_YES/BUY_NO recs and 44,285 NO_TRADE recs — no outcome events logged
Confidence formula: edge_score = min(1.0, (edge_candidate - 0.05) / 0.10 + 0.5), then confidence = edge_score * data_quality * spread_quality
Known outliers that inflate 0.30-0.40 bucket: trades 183/184/185 (lad-tor, conf=0.315, +$236 each), trade 316 (conf=0.39, entry=0.011, +$187)
context.why_this_matters: The current confidence gate (>=0.65) was set to select higher-quality signals. If confidence is actually anti-predictive, the gate is selecting the worst signals. But before concluding this, we must verify whether the inversion survives outlier removal — if lad-tor and the ungated near-resolution entries explain the 0.30-0.40 bucket's outperformance, confidence inversion disappears and the gate is simply operating with insufficient post-gate data.

objective: Determine whether the confidence inversion finding survives outlier removal, characterize the signal distribution in the shadow log, and identify what drives the BUY_NO/BUY_YES PnL asymmetry.
not_allowed: Conclude edge exists or does not exist — only answer the signal quality questions

work_items.question: Does confidence inversion survive outlier removal?
work_items.method: Recompute the confidence bucket table from trades_sports.db, excluding: trades 183, 184, 185 (lad-tor cluster) and trades 310, 316 (ungated near-resolution). Report win rate and avg PnL per bucket on the cleaned sample. State whether the 0.30-0.40 bucket still outperforms 0.60-0.65 after removal.

work_items.question: Is BUY_NO's historical advantage concentrated in outliers?
work_items.method: Recompute BUY_NO vs BUY_YES expectancy excluding the same 5 outlier trades. Report: cleaned BUY_NO n/WR/avg_pnl vs cleaned BUY_YES n/WR/avg_pnl. Determine whether BUY_NO's advantage is structural or explained by the outlier cluster.

work_items.question: What does the confidence distribution look like in the shadow log?
work_items.method: Read shadow_recommendations.jsonl. For all BUY_YES/BUY_NO records (action != 'NO_TRADE'), compute: histogram of confidence values in bins [<0.30, 0.30-0.40, 0.40-0.50, 0.50-0.55, 0.55-0.60, 0.60-0.65, 0.65-0.70, >=0.70]. Report count per bucket. Compare to the confidence distribution of actual trades in the DB to identify whether the gate is functioning as expected (mostly blocking <0.65).

work_items.question: What fraction of shadow BUY_YES/BUY_NO recs have confidence >= 0.65?
work_items.method: From S3 data, report: N recs with confidence >= 0.65 / total BUY/BUY_NO recs. This answers whether the gate is primarily blocking low-confidence recs or high-confidence recs. Also report the median and mean confidence across all actionable shadow recs.

work_items.question: Does the shadow log show BUY_NO vs BUY_YES distribution imbalance?
work_items.method: From shadow_recommendations.jsonl, count BUY_YES vs BUY_NO recs total and at confidence >= 0.65. Compare ratio to actual trades DB ratio (BUY_YES: 195, BUY_NO: 82). If the shadow ratio matches but the outcome ratio diverges, that's a selection artifact. If shadow itself is heavily BUY_YES biased, that's a model-level bias.

work_items.question: Is BUY_NO vs BUY_YES asymmetry explained by inning, score state, or entry price?
work_items.method: From trades_sports.db, break the BUY_NO vs BUY_YES comparison by: (a) entry price bucket (<0.15 vs 0.15-0.22 vs 0.22+), (b) reason_close (take_profit vs stop_loss vs gap_stop vs market_resolved). Report WR and avg_pnl for each cross. The goal is to identify whether BUY_NO wins because of structural market asymmetry (NO prices are cheaper) or because BUY_NO trades happen at different market conditions.

work_items.question: Does the confidence formula saturate in the shadow log?
work_items.method: From shadow_recommendations.jsonl, for BUY_YES/BUY_NO recs, compute: distribution of raw edge values (edge_yes for BUY_YES, edge_no for BUY_NO). Report mean/median/p90 edge. Separately report mean/median confidence. If mean edge is > 0.15 (which would push edge_score to 1.0 regardless), the confidence formula is saturated and data_quality/spread_quality are the dominant drivers of confidence variation, not actual edge.

work_items.question: Can shadow recs be matched to trade outcomes to build a calibration curve?
work_items.method: Attempt to join shadow_recommendations.jsonl BUY_YES/BUY_NO recs to trades_sports.db closed trades by market_slug (approximate match, since market_id formats may differ). For matched pairs: plot (confidence_at_recommendation, trade_won) grouping to build: predicted win probability (confidence) vs actual win rate. Report N matched, unmatched. If N >= 30 matched, report the calibration table. If not enough matches, report why and note this as a gap.
work_items.note: Shadow log has no outcome events. The match must be done via market_slug + approximate timestamp alignment against the DB trade record.

work_items.question: What is the E8 proper random-side baseline?
work_items.method: For every closed trade in the DB with non-zero pnl_usd, compute the counterfactual: what would pnl_usd have been if the side was flipped? BUY_YES at entry_px → counterfactual is BUY_NO at (1-entry_px). For BUY_YES: actual won if pnl > 0. For flipped BUY_NO: won if (1 - entry_px) resolved to YES (i.e., if the original YES side lost). Compute avg counterfactual pnl and compare to avg actual pnl. Report: E8 PASS if actual avg_pnl > counterfactual avg_pnl.

required_sections: S1-S9 answers with method, result numbers, and 1-sentence interpretation each
confidence_inversion_verdict: does inversion survive outlier removal? YES / NO / PARTIALLY
buy_no_advantage_verdict: is BUY_NO advantage structural or outlier-driven? STRUCTURAL / OUTLIER_DRIVEN / AMBIGUOUS
primary_finding: 2-3 sentences — what does the signal quality picture look like after this audit?

constraints: Do not conclude edge exists. This audit answers signal quality questions only.
Do not open any follow-on tasks. Return findings only.
If shadow log matching (S8) is infeasible, say so explicitly and explain why.
All numbers must be computed from data, not inferred from prior audit results.
